en-us.html:
To learn to create, first learn to fix.

Story

I wipe my forehead as I finally release the hydraulic cylinders of my press. The stifling heat of the workshop would leave anyone parched. I grab my canteen and take a few swigs of tepid water to quench my thirst. Near Oddball's charging station, Aberdeen is working hard, her face dripping with sweat. She skillfully tightens the bolts of the last prototype… or what's left of it after last week's explosive disaster. Even though she's young – maybe even younger than I was when I first came to the Foundry – Aby puts in lots of hours without complaining. And judging by my Apprentice's smile when she works, she clearly loves what she does. I can't wait to see her face when she finds out she'll become a Journeyman in a few months, and that this workshop will be hers.

I walk over to her and offer the screwdriver she needs. She takes it without looking and adjusts the angle of rotation of one or two gears. I give her a few instructions and offer a few tips. She nods, eager to learn more. For some time now, we've barely needed to talk. We understand each other like a well-oiled machine. Aby starts to casually hum as she works. Like me, she's clearly eager to see what this new iteration of “FarFetch” can do. But before that, we need to find out why the malfunction happened. Did one of the Kelon cylinders overload? Was it a bad connection? A short circuit? I'll get her a milkshake in the mess when she's finished. And with all the ruckus that the test caused, I'll probably have to buy Bash a pint when he gets back from his trip. It's the least I can do after burning his beard.

blocks.text: To learn to create, first learn to fix.

blocks.title: Story
paragraphs: I wipe my forehead as I finally release the hydraulic cylinders of my press. The stifling heat of the workshop would leave anyone parched. I grab my canteen and take a few swigs of tepid water to quench my thirst. Near Oddball's charging station, Aberdeen is working hard, her face dripping with sweat. She skillfully tightens the bolts of the last prototype… or what's left of it after last week's explosive disaster. Even though she's young – maybe even younger than I was when I first came to the Foundry – Aby puts in lots of hours without complaining. And judging by my Apprentice's smile when she works, she clearly loves what she does. I can't wait to see her face when she finds out she'll become a Journeyman in a few months, and that this workshop will be hers.

I walk over to her and offer the screwdriver she needs. She takes it without looking and adjusts the angle of rotation of one or two gears. I give her a few instructions and offer a few tips. She nods, eager to learn more. For some time now, we've barely needed to talk. We understand each other like a well-oiled machine. Aby starts to casually hum as she works. Like me, she's clearly eager to see what this new iteration of “FarFetch” can do. But before that, we need to find out why the malfunction happened. Did one of the Kelon cylinders overload? Was it a bad connection? A short circuit? I'll get her a milkshake in the mess when she's finished. And with all the ruckus that the test caused, I'll probably have to buy Bash a pint when he gets back from his trip. It's the least I can do after burning his beard.

fr-fr.html:
Pour apprendre à créer, il faut d'abord apprendre à réparer.

Histoire

Je m'essuie le front en relâchant enfin les vérins hydrauliques de ma presse. Il fait une chaleur étouffante au sein de l'atelier, assez pour dessécher n'importe qui. Je saisis ma gourde et bois quelques gorgées d'eau tiède pour me désaltérer. Non loin de la station de charge d'Oddball, Aberdeen est en train de travailler, le visage dégoulinant de sueur. D'une main experte, elle resserre les boulons du dernier prototype, ou du moins ce qu'il en reste après l'explosif désastre de la semaine passée. Même si elle est jeune — peut-être même plus que moi lorsque je suis arrivée à la Fonderie — Aby enchaîne les heures sans rechigner. Et à voir le sourire de mon Apprentie quand elle besogne, elle adore clairement ce qu'elle fait. J'ai hâte de voir sa tête quand elle découvrira que dans quelques mois, elle sera nommée Compagnon, et que cet atelier sera le sien.

Je m'approche et lui tends le tournevis dont elle a besoin. Elle s'en saisit machinalement, et ajuste l'angle de rotation d'un ou deux engrenages. Je lui donne quelques instructions, lui offre quelques conseils. Elle acquiesce, enthousiaste à l'idée d'apprendre. Depuis quelque temps, nous n'avons presque plus besoin de parler, comme une machine bien huilée. Nonchalamment, Aby se met à fredonner en œuvrant. Comme moi, elle est clairement impatiente de voir ce que cette nouvelle itération de "FarFetch" a dans le ventre. Mais avant ça, il va falloir vérifier d'où est venu le dysfonctionnement. La surcharge d'un des cylindres de Kélon ? Un problème de connecteur ? Un court-circuit ? Je lui prendrai un milk-shake au mess quand elle aura fini ça. Et vu tout le tintouin que le test a causé, je vais sûrement devoir payer une pinte à Bash quand il reviendra de sa virée. Je lui dois bien ça pour avoir cramé ses poils de barbe.

blocks.text: Pour apprendre à créer, il faut d'abord apprendre à réparer.
